meaning: adj.固有的,内在的,与生俱来的
examples: Individuality is a valued and inherent part of the British character.
The use of these materials does involve some inherent questions.
Everyone has his inherent ability, which is easily concealed by habits.
At the same time, I will never be completely comfortable with the inherent unpredictability of long-term travel.
Over the past two weeks, I discussed information and incentives issues inherent in Obamacare.
